Regions Artists Timeline All works

Vincent van Gogh

26 works in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
A Peasant Woman Digging in Front of Her Cottage
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · Realism · 1880–1890 · Oil on canvas
Landscape with Wheelbarrow
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1883 · watercolor and opaque watercolor with black chalk on cream paper
Weeping Woman
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1883 · Black and white chalk
Tetards (Pollards)
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1884 · Pen and brown ink
Avenue of Pollard Birches and Poplars
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1884 · Reed pen and iron-gall ink on tan laid paper
The Potato Eaters
by Vincent van Gogh
Early Van Gogh · 1885 · Nuenen
Dark, muddy, deliberately ugly. Van Gogh wrote that he wanted to show hands that had dug the earth eating the potatoes those hands had grown. His breakthrough, though critics hated it.
The Carrot Puller
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1885 · Black chalk
Self-Portrait (Van Gogh)
by Vincent van Gogh
Post-Impressionism · 1887
A Paris self-portrait, jacket gray-green, hat a pale block. Van Gogh painted 35+ self-portraits across his career; this one marks his turn toward the dotted Impressionist brushwork he would soon abandon for thicker, louder color.
Self-Portrait
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · Post-Impressionism · 1887 · Oil on artist's board
Fishing in Spring, the Pont de Clichy (Asnières)
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · Impressionism · 1887 · Oil on canvas
Sunflowers
by Vincent van Gogh
Post-Impressionism · 1888
Painted in Arles to decorate Gauguin's guest bedroom. Van Gogh made seven versions; in letters, he called them an ode to gratitude.
Café Terrace at Night
by Vincent van Gogh
Post-Impressionism · 1888 · Arles
The yellow terrace still stands in Arles (renamed after the painting). No black in the night sky — deep cobalt blue everywhere instead. Van Gogh's first major work done at night.
The Bedroom
by Vincent van Gogh
Post-Impressionism · 1888
Van Gogh's own room at the Yellow House in Arles — painted flat, walls tilted, colors simplified to "rest the mind." Three versions survive; he made the second from memory in a mental hospital.
The Poet's Garden
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · Post-Impressionism · 1888 · Oil on canvas
Landscape at Arles
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1888 · Pen and brown ink over graphite on cream wove paper
The Starry Night
by Vincent van Gogh
Post-Impressionism · Van Gogh · 1889
Painted from the window of his asylum room at Saint-Rémy, from memory. The village below is imagined; the swirling sky he said he could actually see.
The Large Plane Trees (Road Menders at Saint-Rémy)
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1889 · oil on fabric
Two Poplars in the Alpilles near Saint-Rémy
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1889 · oil on fabric
Madame Roulin Rocking the Cradle (La berceuse)
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · Post-Impressionism · 1889 · Oil on canvas
Weeping Tree
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1889 · Reed pen and black-brown ink
Cypresses
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1889 · Pen and reed pen and brown inks and graphite on cream wove paper
Adeline Ravoux
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1890 · oil on fabric
Dr. Gachet
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1890 · Etching and drypoint, enhanced with black ink on laid paper
The Drinkers
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · Post-Impressionism · 1890 · Oil on canvas
Cottages with a Woman Working in the Middle Ground
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1890 · Charcoal
Portrait of Dr. Gachet
by Vincent van Gogh
Netherlands · 1890 · Etching in black on tan wove paper
